is the former longtime editor of *[Fangoria](/wiki/Fangoria "Fangoria")* magazine,[[1]](#cite_note-gallagher-1)[[2]](#cite_note-collis-2) as well as an author about the horror genre, a film festival programmer, and a producer/interviewer for horror-related TV and radio shows.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American journalist

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/d3/Tony_Timpone.gif)](/wiki/File:Tony_Timpone.gif)

Former *[Fangoria](/wiki/Fangoria_Magazine "Fangoria Magazine")* editor Tony Timpone, emceeing a [Weekend of Horrors](/wiki/Weekend_of_Horrors "Weekend of Horrors") convention

**Tony Timpone** is the former longtime editor of *[Fangoria](/wiki/Fangoria "Fangoria")* magazine,[[1]](#cite_note-gallagher-1)[[2]](#cite_note-collis-2) as well as an author about the horror genre, a film festival programmer, and a producer/interviewer for horror-related TV and radio shows.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Timpone&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Tony Timpone was born in New York City, raised in Queens, and graduated from [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University") in 1985.[[3]](#cite_note-NYU_Alumni-3) He wrote for the university's two student newspapers, *The Courier* and *Washington Square News*, and majored in journalism.[[3]](#cite_note-NYU_Alumni-3) As a child, he had read the influential *[Famous Monsters of Filmland](/wiki/Famous_Monsters_of_Filmland "Famous Monsters of Filmland")* magazine, and said he "always wanted to write for a magazine like that."[[3]](#cite_note-NYU_Alumni-3)

In high school, he had been president of a science-fiction club. He attended science-fiction conventions, where he would interview genre stars. Selling his freelance interviews, he launched his journalism career. He wanted to write for *[Fangoria](/wiki/Fangoria "Fangoria")*, but it was not accepting freelance articles at the time:

> However, they had a sister publication which was a monthly science fiction magazine, called *[Starlog](/wiki/Starlog "Starlog")*. While I was still a student at NYU, I was able to sell a few articles to *Starlog,* such as interviews with actors in the *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek")* films. I'd meet these actors at conventions, or if I found out they were going to be performing in a Broadway show, I would write to them and arrange interviews while they were in town. I also sold articles to other niche magazines, like *Monsterland* and *[Enterprise Incidents](/w/index.php?title=Enterprise_Incidents&action=edit&redlink=1 "Enterprise Incidents (page does not exist)")*. But *Fangoria* was the magazine I really wanted to work for.[[3]](#cite_note-NYU_Alumni-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Timpone&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### *Fangoria*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Timpone&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Fangoria")]

Tony Timpone began working with *[Fangoria](/wiki/Fangoria "Fangoria")* magazine in 1985 and continued there until February 2010, when he was succeeded by [Chris Alexander](/wiki/Chris_Alexander_(editor) "Chris Alexander (editor)"). He said of getting the job, starting under editor David McDonnell,[[4]](#cite_note-Shadow_Realm-4) that:

> I first joined the *Fangoria* staff in July of 1985. It was my first job out of college, although I had been freelancing to *Fango'*s sister publication *Starlog* during my last year at college. I began as an Editorial Assistant to then-Editor David Everitt, who decided to move on a month after I started. Since I was fresh out of college and this was my first job in publishing, I was a little too green to take over the job myself. So I served as the Managing Editor to Dave McDonnell, who took over as Editor until I got my wings. The following year, I was promoted to Editor in Chief.[[4]](#cite_note-Shadow_Realm-4)

In the early 1990s, Timpone was a consultant on three *Fangoria* movies for [Columbia TriStar](/wiki/Sony_Pictures_Motion_Picture_Group "Sony Pictures Motion Picture Group") Home Video: *Mindwarp* (1991), *Children of the Night* (1991), and *Severed Ties* (1992).[[5]](#cite_note-Horrorchannel-5) In the early to mid-2000s, he served as an acquisitions chief for *Fangoria* 's various video labels.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In addition, he was a producer/interviewer for Fangoria TV’s *Screamography*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] a recurring guest on the since-defunct [Sirius Satellite Radio](/wiki/Sirius_Satellite_Radio "Sirius Satellite Radio") show *[Fangoria Radio](/wiki/Fangoria_Radio "Fangoria Radio")* with host [Dee Snider](/wiki/Dee_Snider "Dee Snider") and co-host [Debbie Rochon](/wiki/Debbie_Rochon "Debbie Rochon"), for four years beginning in 2006,[[6]](#cite_note-All_Access-6)[[7]](#cite_note-Syfy-7) and the co-producer/programming producer/moderator of *Fangoria'*s [Weekend of Horrors](/wiki/Weekend_of_Horrors "Weekend of Horrors") conventions from 1986 to 2009, and again beginning 2021[[8]](#cite_note-Creation_Entertainment-8)[[9]](#cite_note-MovieWeb-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Monsterpalooza-10)

After leaving his editor-in-chief position in 2010, Timpone began running *Fangoria'*s video-on-demand and DVD divisions.[[7]](#cite_note-Syfy-7)

Timpone also edited *Fangoria'*s sister publication *[Gorezone](/wiki/Gorezone "Gorezone")*, which lasted from 1989 to 1993.[[5]](#cite_note-Horrorchannel-5)

### Other work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Timpone&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Other work")]

Since 1993, Timpone has helped program international horror/fantasy festivals in [Milan, Italy](/wiki/Milan,_Italy "Milan, Italy") and [Montreal, Quebec](/wiki/Montreal,_Quebec "Montreal, Quebec"), Canada. Before its closing, Timpone programmed and hosted The Two Boots Pioneer Theater's monthly "Monster Mondays" film series.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Under the name Anthony Timpone, he authored the 1996 [St. Martin's Press](/wiki/St._Martin%27s_Press "St. Martin's Press") book *Men, Makeup, and Monsters: Hollywood's Masters of Illusion and FX*.[[11]](#cite_note-Anaheim_Public_Library-11)

In late 2004, he served as a producer on [Bravo](/wiki/Bravo_(US_TV_channel) "Bravo (US TV channel)")’s five-hour documentary series *[The 100 Scariest Movie Moments](/wiki/The_100_Scariest_Movie_Moments "The 100 Scariest Movie Moments")*: "Kaufman Films needed a 'horror guy' to get them connected with the folks in the horror industry. So I came on board and tracked down and interviewed most of the scream greats for the series."[[5]](#cite_note-Horrorchannel-5)

He was programming director and moderator for the horror conventions [Monsterpalooza](/wiki/Monsterpalooza "Monsterpalooza") and Son of Monsterpalooza in at least 2016 and 2017.[[10]](#cite_note-Monsterpalooza-10) Since 1998, he has been the co-director of international programming for Montreal, Canada's [Fantasia Film Festival](/wiki/Fantasia_Film_Festival "Fantasia Film Festival").[[7]](#cite_note-Syfy-7)
